data.title: XRP Price: How ETF Approval in October Could Push It Back Toward $5
data.description:
Bitcoin slipped more than 2% after failing to build on recent gains, and Ethereum followed with a similar drop.
source: https://coindoo.com/xrp-price-how-etf-approval-in-october-could-push-it-back-toward-5/

data.title: The largest individual holder of WLFI transferred another 100 million WLFI, worth $18.24 million
data.description: According to Ai Yi, an on-chain analyst, moonmanifest 47.eth transferred another 100 million WLFI to the new address 0 x 6 E 7...4 d 7 c 4 10 minutes ago, with a value of $18.24 million. Earlier news reported that the top individual holder of WLFI distributed 79.54 million tokens to 9 addresses.

data.title: First Dogecoin ETF May Debut in the US Next Week: Bloomberg Analyst
data.description:
A Dogecoin exchange-traded fund (ETF) could be hitting U. S. markets as soon as next week, according to Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/first-dogecoin-etf-may-debut-in-the-us-next-week-bloomberg-analyst/

data.title: Ethereum's Market Outlook Remains Stagnant, Analyst Predicts
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, cryptocurrency analyst @IamCryptoWolf has shared insights on Ethereum's current market status. The analyst noted that, as previously anticipated, Ethereum (ETH) lacks significant movement on the charts aside from the usual fear, uncertainty, and doubt (FUD). "ETH remains in a dull phase, and this is expected to continue for several weeks," the analyst stated. The basic scenario suggests that Ethereum might reach a peak before the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting in two weeks, followed by a 'sell the news' event, eventually hitting a low point.

@IamCryptoWolf has been sharing analysis on the Tradingview platform since 2014 and currently has 111,000 followers on the X platform.

data.title: Justin Sun publishes a long article calling on World Liberty Financials to unlock its tokens
data.description: Justin Sun, in a post on the X platform, stated that as an early and major investor in World Liberty Financials, his tokens had been unreasonably frozen. He demanded that the project team unlock his tokens. Sun stated that a great financial brand must be built on fairness, transparency, and trust. The unilateral freezing of investor assets not only violates the legitimate rights and interests of investors but also risks undermining broader confidence in World Liberty Financials.

data.title: Hoskinson Demands Apology After $600M Embezzlement Audit Clears Him
data.description:
Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson lashes out at the Cardano Foundation after an audit cleared him of $600M embezzlement claims.

The post Hoskinson Demands Apology After $600M Embezzlement Audit Clears Him first appeared on Ecoinimist.

source: https://ecoinimist.com/2025/09/05/hoskinson-demands-apology-600-m-audit/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=hoskinson-demands-apology-600-m-audit

data.title: Sun Yuchen retweeted Nansen CEO’s tweet defending him and claimed to be “innocent”
data.description: Justin Sun retweeted Nansen CEO Alex Svanevik’s tweet about “Analysis of Justin Sun’s WLFI token address activity” and said, “I am innocent.” Alex Svanevik said in the tweet, “After confirming with the agent: Justin Sun did not cause the decline of WLFI.”

data.title: The largest individual holder of WLFI transferred 79.54 million WLFI to 9 addresses, worth $14.69 million
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, over the past 18 hours, the largest individual WLFI holder, moonmanifest.eth (0 x 7 e 0...90 b 8), distributed 79.54 million WLFI (approximately $14.69 million) to nine new addresses. One of these receiving addresses, moonmanifestphilanthropy.eth, had previously made a small sale three days ago. Currently, there has been no further selling activity from these receiving addresses. This holder holds 1% of the total WLFI supply and unlocked 200 million tokens during the TGE. They are suspected to be TechnoRevenant, a key participant in the XPL hedging incident.

data.title: Nate Geraci suggests that former SEC Chairman Gensler's missing text messages during the FTX debacle may not be a coincidence
data.description: Nate Geraci, president of The ETF Store, posted on the X platform that text messages from former SEC Chairman Gensler between October 2022 and September 2023 were lost. This period covers events such as the FTX collapse and the Grayscale spot Bitcoin ETF lawsuit. He hinted that this might not be a coincidence, writing in the comments section, "One day I will go public with my conspiracy theory..."

data.title: Since 2025, 154 publicly listed companies in the United States have announced plans to raise approximately $98.4 billion to purchase cryptocurrencies.
data.description: Since January, 154 publicly listed companies have announced plans to raise approximately $98.4 billion to purchase cryptocurrencies, according to the Financial Times, citing data from Architect Partners. This figure represents a significant increase from the $33.6 billion raised by just 10 companies before 2025.

data.title: Bithumb will list EUL and Korean Won trading pairs
data.description: According to the official announcement, Bithumb will launch the EUL Korean won trading pair.

data.title: Binance Alpha will suspend AIO trading on September 8 to support contract swaps
data.description: Binance Alpha will support BEP 20 contract swaps for OLAXBT (AIO). Starting at 05:00 (UTC) on September 8, 2025, Binance will suspend AIO trading to support contract swaps. Trading of OLAXBT (AIO) via BEP 20 is expected to resume at 08:00 UTC on the same day.

data.title: South Korea's financial regulators have issued guidelines for virtual asset lending services, prohibiting excessive leverage lending.
data.description: South Korea's financial regulators have issued their first guidelines for virtual asset lending services. Due to increased competition among exchanges and heightened investor risks, regulators have completely banned leveraged and cash lending, and set individual limits and fee caps to discourage short-selling-like practices. On the 5th, the Financial Services Commission (FSC) announced it would implement the self-regulatory "Virtual Asset Lending Guidelines," developed by the Financial Supervisory Service and DAXA. The new guidelines focus on three key areas: service scope restrictions, user protection, and market stability. The guidelines explicitly prohibit excessive leveraged lending and Korean won cash lending, require exchanges to use their own assets for services, and prohibit third-party entrusted or indirect lending models. To strengthen user protections, first-time users must complete DAXA's online education and aptitude test. Lending limits will be set between 30 million and 70 million won, depending on trading experience. Preemptive notification of forced liquidation risks is required, and margin calls are permitted. The annual fee rate cannot exceed 20%, and public disclosure of lending status and liquidation cases for each currency is mandatory. In terms of market stabilization measures, lending targets are limited to the top 20 assets by market capitalization or listed on three or more Korean won exchanges, excluding trading warning products and currencies suspected of abnormal transactions; and the establishment of an internal control mechanism is required to prevent market fluctuations caused by excessive concentration of specific currencies.

data.title: HackQuest acquires former Gitcoin hackathon platform BuidlBox
data.description: According to Hackernoon, HackQuest, a Web 3 developer education and hackathon platform, announced its full acquisition of the hackathon and bounty platform BuidlBox. BuidlBox, spawned from the Gitcoin hackathon platform, has attracted over 50,000 developers and a strong developer base in the US and Europe. It has hosted world-renowned hackathons such as the Permissionless Hackathon and the ETH Denver Hackathon. The HackQuest team stated that this acquisition is a significant step in building a "global developer network." Leveraging BuidlBox's user base and brand influence, HackQuest will further expand into the European and American developer markets and enhance its global developer ecosystem.

data.title: The crypto sector fell back across the board, with the DeFi sector leading the decline by over 5%, and ETH once fell below $4,300
data.description: On September 5th, according to SoSoValue data, the crypto sector experienced a correction across the board. The DeFi sector fell 5.22% in the 24-hour period. Within the sector, World Liberty Financial (WLFI) fell 15.15%, and Ethena (ENA) dropped 10.10%. Additionally, Bitcoin (BTC) fell 0.76%, fluctuating in a narrow range around $110,000. Ethereum (ETH) dropped 3.20%, briefly falling below $4,300. Elsewhere, the CeFi sector fell 1.04% over the past 24 hours, with Bitget token (BGB) down 3.65%. The PayFi sector fell 1.14%, but Monero (XMR) saw a slight increase of 0.54%. The Layer 2 sector fell 1.72%, with Mantle (MNT) and ex-MATIC (POL) surging intraday by 4.30% and 1.98% respectively. The Meme sector fell 1.96%, with MemeCore (M) continuing its sharp rise, gaining 28.42% over the past 24 hours, and Pump.fun (PUMP) rising 6.11%. The Layer 1 sector fell 2.11%. Crypto sector indices, which reflect historical market trends, show that the ssiDeFi, ssiNFT, and ssiAI indices fell 5.61%, 4.13%, and 3.25%, respectively.

data.title: Gate has launched Union (U) and launched the U series "family bucket" event
data.description: According to an official announcement, Gate has launched Union (U). To reward its users, Gate is launching a series of events, including a contract trading competition, Yubibao, and Launchpool. The Union (U) contract trading competition will run from 21:00 on September 4th to 00:00 on September 12th (UTC+8). Users who complete their first trade, a trading sprint, or refer a friend will receive a share of 1,000,000 U. Gate will also launch the Union (U) Yubibao, where users can share a prize of 2,500,000 U on a first-come, first-served basis. The 311th Gate Launchpool event will run from 17:00 on September 5th to 17:00 on September 15th (UTC+8). Users who stake Gemini Dollars (GUSD) and U will receive a share of 6,500,000 U. In addition, BountyDrop and Alpha activities will be launched soon, so stay tuned.

data.title: Digital asset infrastructure company Pointsville completes Series A funding round, with Tether participating
data.description: Pointsville, a digital asset infrastructure company, has completed its Series A funding round, led by Valor Capital Group, with participation from Tether, Temasek-backed Superscrypt, SNZ, Credit Saison, K2 Integrity, Citrino, and Dynamo. The specific amount of funding has not yet been disclosed, and the new funds are intended to be used for the company's asset digitization and RWA transformation.

data.title: Yunfeng Financial: In addition to ETH, we also plan to include BTC, SOL, etc. into the company's strategic reserve assets
data.description: Yunfeng Financial previously purchased a total of 10,000 ETH on the open market. Alibaba founder Jack Ma and Yu Feng, chairman of Yunfeng Financial Group, co-founded Yunfeng Fund in 2010. Yunfeng Financial stated that the newly purchased ETH will be listed as an investment asset in its financial statements and that it will continue to increase its investment in digital assets. In addition to Ethereum, it plans to explore the inclusion of diversified mainstream digital assets, including Bitcoin (BTC) and Solana (SOL), in its strategic reserve assets. (Caixin.com)

data.title: Over the past two months, the two addresses have accumulated 22,753 MKR, worth approximately $39.62 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, two addresses received a total of 22,753 MKR from FalconX over the past two months. Address 0xb2c7 received 14,000 MKR worth $24.25 million six hours ago, while address 0xc230 received 8,753 MKR worth $15.37 million over the past two months.

data.title: Thumzup, a company owned by Trump's eldest son, plans to transform into a DOGE mining company and purchase 3,500 DOGE mining machines.
data.description: Thumzup (TZUP), a media company owned by Donald Trump's eldest son, released a shareholder letter announcing its intention to acquire DogeHash Technologies to establish a DOGE mining operation, completing its strategic transformation into a leading cryptocurrency mining company. The company has invested $1 million in Bitcoin, and its board of directors has authorized an increase in holdings of cryptocurrencies including DOGE, LTC, SOL, XRP, ETH, and USDC. It has also authorized the purchase of 2,500 DOGE mining machines, with the potential purchase of an additional 1,000 machines. Thumzup (TZUP) previously announced that it will acquire DogeHash Technologies in an all-stock transaction. The company will be renamed "Dogehash Technologies Holdings, Inc." and trade on the Nasdaq under the new ticker symbol "XDOG." The transaction is expected to close by the end of the year.
